summaryText: Pastor Darrison Tellez opened the service with excitement and led the congregation into a message from Matthew 25:1-13, the parable of the ten virgins. He used personal stories from his youth—like attending church camps, starting a worship band, and meeting his wife—to illustrate a passionate, 'on-fire' faith that can diminish over time. The central question was about maintaining spiritual fervor: 'What has stolen your fire?' Pastor Darrison taught that the 'oil' in the parable represents the sustaining presence of God, which we must protect and replenish. He outlined three principles: don't spoil your oil by avoiding life's pressures, don't spill your oil by carelessly giving away what is meant to sustain you, and don't forsake your oil by forgetting your first love for Jesus. The message emphasized that personal encounters with God fuel our light, and we must actively guard our relationship with Him to be ready for His return. The call was to return to our first love, repent, and ask God for fresh oil to reignite our passion and purpose in 2025.
mainPoints: Guard your spiritual passion ('oil') by not letting life's pressures spoil it; God uses pressing seasons to produce fuel for your light.
Protect your personal faith by not spilling your oil on wrong things; your relationship with God cannot be borrowed and must be personally maintained.
Reignite your first love for Jesus by not forsaking your oil; return to your initial passion through repentance and fresh encounters with God.

versesMentioned.text: At that time the kingdom of heaven will be like ten virgins who took their lamps and went out to meet the bridegroom. Five of them were foolish and five were wise. The foolish ones took their lamps but did not take any oil with them. The wise ones, however, took oil in jars along with their lamps. The bridegroom was a long time in coming, and they all became drowsy and fell asleep. At midnight the cry rang out: ‘Here’s the bridegroom! Come out to meet him!’ Then all the virgins woke up and trimmed their lamps. The foolish ones said to the wise, ‘Give us some of your oil; our lamps are going out.’ ‘No,’ they replied, ‘there may not be enough for both us and you. Instead, go to those who sell oil and buy some for yourselves.’ But while they were on their way to buy the oil, the bridegroom arrived. The virgins who were ready went in with him to the wedding banquet. And the door was shut. Later the others also came. ‘Lord, Lord,’ they said, ‘open the door for us!’ But he replied, ‘Truly I tell you, I don’t know you.’ Therefore keep watch, because you do not know the day or the hour.

Then Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane, and he said to them, ‘Sit here while I go over there and pray.’ He took Peter and the two sons of Zebedee along with him, and he began to be sorrowful and troubled. Then he said to them, ‘My soul is overwhelmed with sorrow to the point of death. Stay here and keep watch with me.’ Going a little farther, he fell with his face to the ground and prayed, ‘My Father, if it is possible, may this cup be taken from me. Yet not as I will, but as you will.’

You are the light of the world. A town built on a hill cannot be hidden. Neither do people light a lamp and put it under a bowl. Instead they put it on its stand, and it gives light to everyone in the house. In the same way, let your light shine before others, that they may see your good deeds and glorify your Father in heaven.

Then he turned toward the woman and said to Simon, ‘Do you see this woman? I came into your house. You did not give me any water for my feet, but she wet my feet with her tears and wiped them with her hair. You did not give me a kiss, but this woman, from the time I entered, has not stopped kissing my feet. You did not put oil on my head, but she has poured perfume on my feet. Therefore, I tell you, her many sins have been forgiven—as her great love has shown. But whoever has been forgiven little loves little.’
You, LORD, keep my lamp burning; my God turns my darkness into light.

Yet I hold this against you: You have forsaken the love you had at first. Consider how far you have fallen! Repent and do the things you did at first. If you do not repent, I will come to you and remove your lampstand from its place.

Monday.scripture: Matthew 5:14-16 - 'You are the light of the world... let your light shine...'
Monday.focus: Recognizing your role as a light.
Monday.action: Identify one situation today where you can intentionally reflect God's love and hope.
Monday.prayer: Lord, help me to see where I can shine for You today and give me the courage to do so.

Tuesday.scripture: Matthew 26:39 - '...yet not as I will, but as you will.'
Tuesday.focus: Submitting to God's will in pressure.
Tuesday.action: When you feel pressured today, pause and pray a simple prayer of surrender: 'God, not my will, but Yours.'
Tuesday.prayer: Father, in moments of stress, remind me that You are pressing out purpose. I submit to Your process.

Wednesday.scripture: Psalm 18:28 - 'You, LORD, keep my lamp burning...'
Wednesday.focus: Depending on God as your source.
Wednesday.action: Spend 5 minutes in worship or prayer without asking for anything, simply acknowledging God as your sustainer.
Wednesday.prayer: Jesus, You alone keep my flame alive. I depend on You, not my circumstances, to fuel my faith.

Thursday.scripture: Revelation 2:5 - 'Consider how far you have fallen! Repent and do the things you did at first.'
Thursday.action: Think of one simple, passionate act you did for God when you were new in faith (e.g., sharing your testimony, extended prayer) and do it again this week.
Thursday.prayer: God, forgive me for letting my passion cool. Restore the joy of my salvation and my first love for You.

Friday.scripture: Luke 7:47 - '...her many sins have been forgiven—as her great love has shown.'
Friday.action: Find a private moment to express your love for Jesus in a tangible way—write Him a thank-you note, sing a song, or simply sit in grateful silence.
Friday.prayer: Lord, thank You for Your forgiveness. Let my love for You overflow in worship and gratitude today.

Saturday.scripture: Matthew 25:10 - 'The virgins who were ready went in with him to the wedding banquet.'
Saturday.action: Prepare for tomorrow's worship by reflecting: What distraction or attitude do I need to set aside to be fully present with God?
Saturday.prayer: Holy Spirit, search my heart. Prepare me to meet with You. Make me ready and eager for Your presence.
